source: Buy-side diligence question list (demo). Pillars and questions grounded in the topics raised on Apple's Q2 FY26 earnings call (Apr 30, 2026).
thesis: AAPL enters the print mid-supercycle: the iPhone 17 family is driving 20%+ revenue growth — the strongest cycle in company history — while Mac demand (MacBook Neo, plus mini/Studio as local-AI machines) runs ahead of supply. Growth is gated by supply, not demand: the binding constraint is advanced-node SOC availability (iPhone in the March quarter, shifting to Mac into June). The central risk is the memory-cost overhang, modest now but building into a growing gross-margin headwind beyond June. Services keeps compounding at high margin with advertising (App Store, now Maps) as a fresh lever; capital allocation is shifting as Apple drops the net-cash-neutral target and lifts buyback authorization by $100B; AI strategy pairs a Google partnership with in-house foundational models on accelerating R&D; and management hands the CEO role to John Ternus in September.

questions.pillar: Supply-gated hardware supercycle: iPhone and Mac demand is outrunning supply, with broad geographic strength
questions.question: How strong and durable is the iPhone 17 cycle — the growth rate, its drivers, and is it supply-gated demand rather than a pull-forward?
complete_when: Gives the iPhone growth rate and what's driving it (the 17 family, upgrader record)
Speaks to durability — supply-gated demand vs a demand pull-forward
Addresses breadth of demand across major markets

expected.note_models: iPhone +~22%, the strongest cycle in company history, led by the 17 family with a March-quarter record for upgraders and ~99% US satisfaction; double-digit growth across most tracked markets
expected.our_view: Genuine supply-gated demand, not a pull-forward
expected.watch: Upgrader-vs-switcher mix and whether durability holds past the launch quarter

notes: iPhone +22% YoY, strongest cycle in company history — Q2 FY26 call
Driven by the iPhone 17 family; double-digit growth across most tracked markets — Q2 FY26 call
New March-quarter record for upgraders; ~99% US satisfaction — Q2 FY26 call
Base case models iPhone +~22%, ASP/mix-led off the 17 Pro/Air — Vantage note

questions.pillar: Supply-gated hardware supercycle: iPhone and Mac demand is outrunning supply, with broad geographic strength
questions.question: Where do the supply constraints bind, what is the root cause, and when do they ease — iPhone versus Mac?
complete_when: Identifies which products are constrained and when (iPhone in the March quarter, Mac into June)
Names the root cause (advanced-node SOC availability, not memory)
Gives a path to balance / rebalancing timeline

expected.note_models: Advanced-node SOC availability is the binding constraint; iPhone-led in the March quarter, shifting to Mac (mini, Studio, MacBook Neo) into June; mini/Studio several months to rebalance
expected.our_view: The constraint is process nodes, not memory
expected.watch: Whether iPhone is still called constrained into June

notes: Binding constraint is advanced-node SOC availability, not memory — Q2 FY26 call
March quarter constrained primarily on iPhone, less on Mac — Q2 FY26 call
June-quarter constraint shifts to Mac (mini, Studio, Neo); mini/Studio several months to rebalance — Q2 FY26 call

questions.pillar: Supply-gated hardware supercycle: iPhone and Mac demand is outrunning supply, with broad geographic strength
questions.question: How broad is demand across the Mac line (MacBook Neo, new-to-Mac) and geographically (Greater China, India)?
complete_when: Addresses the Mac / MacBook Neo cycle and new-to-Mac customers
Addresses Greater China
Addresses India

expected.note_models: MacBook Neo off the charts and supply-constrained, with a March-quarter record for customers new to Mac; Greater China first half +33% (March-Q +28%, a quarterly record); India the #2 smartphone / #3 PC market at still-modest share with a long runway
expected.our_view: China re-accelerating, India the structural multi-year story
expected.watch: Whether China strength is durable and India share gains accelerate

notes: MacBook Neo off the charts; March-quarter record for customers new to Mac — Q2 FY26 call
Greater China first half +33%, March-Q revenue +28% (a quarterly record) — Q2 FY26 call
India the #2 smartphone / #3 PC market at still-modest share — Q2 FY26 call

questions.pillar: Margin and capital: the memory-cost overhang on gross margin against a shift in the capital-return framework
questions.question: Where is gross margin running and what is the sequential bridge (mix, tariffs, memory, deleverage, FX)?
complete_when: States company and product gross margin
Walks the sequential bridge — mix, tariffs, memory, seasonal deleverage, FX

expected.note_models: Company GM ~49% (March-Q 49.3%); products GM −~200bps sequentially on seasonal deleverage + memory; overall +~110bps on favorable mix and lower tariffs; FX roughly neutral
expected.our_view: Favorable mix and tariff relief are masking the memory bill
expected.bear: Strip the mix/tariff help and the underlying product margin is already softening

notes: March-quarter company gross margin 49.3% — AAPL 10-Q
Products GM −200bps sequentially on seasonal deleverage + memory — Q2 FY26 call
Overall GM +110bps sequentially on favorable mix + lower tariffs; FX neutral — Q2 FY26 call
Tariff relief from IEEPA + Section 122; refunds to be reinvested in US — Q2 FY26 call

questions.pillar: Margin and capital: the memory-cost overhang on gross margin against a shift in the capital-return framework
questions.question: What is the memory-cost trajectory beyond June, how big is the headwind, and can pricing offset it?
complete_when: Characterizes the memory-cost trajectory beyond June (direction and magnitude)
Addresses the range of options / pricing pass-through

expected.note_models: Memory minimal in December, higher in March (offset by inventory), significantly higher in the June guide and increasing beyond; management will weigh a range of options
expected.our_view: A building ~150-200bps gross-margin headwind that compresses company GM toward ~46% in the back half of FY26; we model less than half passed through on price
expected.bear: If memory is framed as 'minimal' or fully offset, the headwind is being downplayed

notes: Memory minimal in Dec Q, higher in March (offset by inventory) — Q2 FY26 call
Significantly higher in the June guide, with increasing impact beyond — Q2 FY26 call
Management will weigh a 'range of options' — Q2 FY26 call
We model a ~150-200bps GM headwind, <half passed through on price — Vantage note

questions.pillar: Margin and capital: the memory-cost overhang on gross margin against a shift in the capital-return framework
questions.question: What does dropping the net-cash-neutral target and the $100B buyback increase signal for capital return?
complete_when: Explains the move off net-cash-neutral (cash and debt evaluated independently)
States the buyback authorization and overall return posture

expected.note_models: Net-cash-neutral retired as a formal target; cash and debt now evaluated independently; +$100B buyback authorization on top of leftover capacity; over $1T returned to date (>$850B in buybacks)
expected.our_view: Added flexibility, not a buyback retreat
expected.watch: Whether the framework change presages a leverage step-up

notes: Net-cash-neutral retired as a formal target; cash and debt evaluated independently — Q2 FY26 call
+$100B buyback authorization on top of leftover capacity — Q2 FY26 call
Over $1T returned to date, >$850B via buybacks — Q2 FY26 call

questions.pillar: The forward call: high-margin Services and advertising, an in-house-plus-Google AI strategy, and the CEO handoff to John Ternus
questions.question: How durable are Services and the advertising lever — services margin, App Store ads, and Apple Maps ads?
complete_when: Addresses Services growth / gross margin
Addresses advertising (App Store search ads and upcoming Apple Maps ads)

expected.note_models: Services gross margin +~20bps sequentially, primarily mix; advertising up YoY with new App Store search-results inventory and Apple Maps ads launching in the US and Canada this summer
expected.our_view: Services is the high-margin compounder and advertising the next lever
expected.watch: Whether Maps ad inventory moves the needle

notes: Services gross margin +20bps sequentially, primarily mix — Q2 FY26 call
Advertising up YoY with new App Store search-results inventory — Q2 FY26 call
Apple Maps ads launching in US and Canada this summer — Q2 FY26 call

questions.pillar: The forward call: high-margin Services and advertising, an in-house-plus-Google AI strategy, and the CEO handoff to John Ternus
questions.question: What is Apple's AI / foundational-model strategy (Google vs in-house) and the investment behind it?
complete_when: Addresses the AI strategy — Google collaboration vs in-house foundational models
Addresses the R&D / investment level and how it is framed

expected.note_models: Two-pronged: a Google collaboration going well plus in-house foundational models; 'investing more,' R&D accelerating faster than the company overall; framed as incremental investment on top of the normal roadmap
expected.our_view: A near-term margin headwind; the Google dependence signals in-house models aren't yet competitive
expected.watch: Whether Google dependence deepens or AI yields a new form factor

notes: Two-pronged: Google collaboration plus in-house foundational models — Q2 FY26 call
'Investing more'; R&D accelerating faster than the company overall — Q2 FY26 call
Google search-default payment sits at antitrust risk under the DOJ remedy — DOJ/Google (NPR)

questions.pillar: The forward call: high-margin Services and advertising, an in-house-plus-Google AI strategy, and the CEO handoff to John Ternus
questions.question: How clean is the CEO transition to John Ternus, and does strategy and capital discipline carry over?
complete_when: Addresses the transition timing
Addresses continuity of strategy and financial discipline

expected.note_models: John Ternus transitions into the CEO role in September; Tim Cook has about one more earnings call; messaging stresses continuity of strategy and financial discipline
expected.watch: Any posture change under the new CEO

notes: John Ternus transitions into the CEO role in September — Q2 FY26 call
Tim Cook has about one more earnings call — Q2 FY26 call
Messaging stresses continuity of strategy and financial discipline — Q2 FY26 call
